servinfo.title: Passenger Air Bag Deactivation Switch Description
servinfo.ptxt: The passenger air bag deactivation switch (1) is installed in the glove box. It is used to suspend the operation of passenger air bag (inflator) module for such cases as the child restraint is installed to the front passenger’s seat.

SDM determines whether to enable or disable operation of passenger air bag (inflator) module based on the air bag deactivation switch setting (Air bag ON / Air bag OFF). The deactivation switch setting can be changed in any ignition switch position or ignition mode.

When ignition is set to “ON”, the SDM detects the setting position (Air bag ON / Air bag OFF) of the passenger air bag deactivation switch and determines whether to enable or disable operation of the passenger air bag (inflator) module. If deactivation switch is in Air bag OFF position, it turns on the passenger air bag deactivation indicator (2) between the center ventilation louvers of the instrument panel to indicate that passenger air bag (inflator) module is disabled.
In addition, even if the passenger air bag deactivation switch setting is changed while ignition is “ON”, SDM does not store any DTC and does not turn on the air bag warning light.

attention1.ptxt: Neglecting to set the passenger air bag deactivation switch as follows will cause a critical injury to the passenger.

item.ptxt: When using the child restraint installed to the front passenger’s seat, set the passenger air bag deactivation switch to the “Air bag OFF” (disable mode) position.
When the child restraint is removed from the front passenger’s seat, set the passenger air bag deactivation switch to the “Air bag ON” (enable mode) position.

servinfosub.title: Enable Mode
servinfosub.ptxt: When ignition is set to “ON” while the passenger air bag deactivation switch (1) is at the “Air bag ON” position (contact position is closed), the passenger air bag deactivation indicator (2) is activated for 6.0 seconds (on for 4 seconds and off for 2 seconds) for the light check, the “Air bag ON signal” is fed to SDM (3), which then turns off the passenger air bag deactivation indicator (2) and the system enters the enable mode. In the enable mode, the air bag system is controlled in the usual manner.

servinfosub.title: Disable Mode
servinfosub.ptxt: When ignition is set to “ON” while the passenger air bag deactivation switch (1) is at the “Air bag OFF” position (contact point is opened), the passenger air bag deactivation indicator (2) is activated for 6.0 seconds (on for 4 seconds and off for 2 seconds) for the light check just like in the enable mode, the “Air bag OFF signal” is fed to SDM (3), which then turns on the passenger air bag deactivation indicator (2) and the system enters the disable mode. In the disable mode, the passenger air bag (inflator) module (4) is always disabled.

servinfosub.title: Fail Safe Operation
servinfosub.ptxt: When a trouble occurs and it is not possible to judge the condition of the passenger air bag deactivation switch (1), SDM (3) makes the passenger air bag deactivation indicator (2) flash (at 0.5 sec. intervals) after the light check to warn occurrence of an abnormality. The operation mode of the passenger air bag (inflator) module depends on the condition when the trouble occurred.

entry.ptxt: Passenger air bag deactivation switch position
